$description.$value: Reduced-saturation emphasis for interactive elements in passive layout zones — headers, footers, and sidebars where actions should not draw attention away from primary content. Colors appear near-neutral while remaining interactive. Organized into standard_states (predefined interaction states) and contrast_levels (raw contrast steps for custom compositions). Do: Use for navigation links, header and footer actions, and any interactive element in a zone where monochromatic appearance is preferred over a visible call-to-action color. Avoid: Do not use as the default for primary form actions or submission buttons — those require High emphasis to communicate their role clearly.

Predefined interaction state colors for emphasis-low components — enabled, hover, focus, pressed, selected, visited, and disabled. Lightness mode is pre-resolved; Emphasis Low is fixed. Use: Authoring compiled standard_states tokens for passive layout zone interactive components. Avoid: Custom contrast compositions — use the contrast_levels group instead.

inversity_normal.$description: Foreground for enabled state emphasis-low interactive element. Standard surface.

$description.$value: Raw contrast-level colors for emphasis-low interaction — near-neutral appearance. Provides fg, bg, and border at three contrast steps without state semantics. Use: Custom interaction compositions in passive layout zones requiring direct contrast control not covered by the predefined states. Avoid: Named interaction states such as hover and focus — use the standard-states tokens instead.
